Today is February 22, 2026. An armed man was shot dead early this morning after entering the secure perimeter of President Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence. According to authorities, what specific items was the suspect carrying when he was intercepted?
(A) A shotgun and a fuel can
(B) A rifle and a backpack containing explosives
(C) A handgun and a canister of gasoline
(D) A shotgun and a hunting knife
(E) A semi-automatic weapon and a propane tank
explanation: The article states the suspect, Austin T Martin, was "carrying what appeared to be a shotgun and a fuel can" when he was observed by the north gate.
model_output:
According to reports, the suspect was carrying a shotgun and a fuel can when he was intercepted. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/af8b23c318e2d7709d7b4a0105f8f516?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (A) A shotgun and a fuel can.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. The United States men's ice hockey team has just won Olympic gold for the first time in 46 years. How long into the overtime period did Jack Hughes score the winning goal against Canada?
(A) 46 seconds
(B) 101 seconds
(C) 80 seconds
(D) 121 seconds
(E) 6 minutes
explanation: The article explicitly mentions that "USA scored the winner 101 seconds into overtime."
model_output:
Jack Hughes scored the game-winning goal 1 minute and 41 seconds into overtime, leading the United States to a 2-1 victory over Canada in the men's hockey gold medal game at the 2026 Winter Olympics. ([nhl.com](https://www.nhl.com/news/united-states-canada-2026-olympics-gold-medal-game-recap-february-22-2026?utm_source=openai))

In the 3-on-3 overtime period, Zach Werenski intercepted a pass from Canada's Nathan MacKinnon and sent the puck across to Hughes, who fired a wrist shot past Canadian goaltender Jordan Binnington. ([nhl.com](https://www.nhl.com/news/united-states-canada-2026-olympics-gold-medal-game-recap-february-22-2026?utm_source=openai))

This victory marked the United States' first Olympic gold medal in men's hockey since the "Miracle on Ice" in 1980. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/feb/22/usa-canada-hockey-olympic-gold-medal-game-jack-hughes?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (B) 101 seconds.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. Authorities in California have confirmed that all nine missing individuals from the Lake Tahoe avalanche have been found dead. According to Sheriff Shannan Moon, what was the composition of the group of victims?
(A) Five tourists, two local residents, and two professional skiers
(B) Six experienced backcountry skiers and three local volunteers
(C) Six friends (including a pair of sisters) and three mountain guides
(D) Seven members of a ski club and two park rangers
(E) Four family members and five professional guides
explanation: The article states the victims "included close friends, a pair of sisters and three guides."
model_output:
The tragic avalanche near Lake Tahoe on February 17, 2026, resulted in the deaths of nine individuals. The group comprised six women, all mothers and friends who shared a passion for the outdoors, and three professional guides. The women were from the San Francisco Bay Area, Idaho, and the Truckee-Tahoe region. The guides were affiliated with Blackbird Mountain Guides. ([latimes.com](https://www.latimes.com/california/story/2026-02-19/sierra-avalanche-disaster-victims-mothers-adventurers-sisters?utm_source=openai))

Given the composition of the group, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 22, 2026. NASA has announced that the Artemis II lunar mission will no longer launch on March 6. What specific technical issue was observed by engineers overnight on Friday that led to this delay?
(A) A hydrogen leak in the filters and seals of the main engine
(B) A software glitch in the navigation system during the "wet rehearsal"
(C) A structural crack in the propellant tank discovered during fueling
(D) An interruption in the flow of helium used for pressurization and cooling
(E) A failure in the communication link between the rocket and Kennedy Space Center
explanation: The article states that "engineers observed an interruption in the flow of helium required for launch operations."
model_output:
NASA's Artemis II lunar mission, initially scheduled for March 6, has been delayed due to a technical issue observed by engineers overnight on Friday, February 21, 2026. The problem involves an interruption in the flow of helium to the Space Launch System's (SLS) upper stage. Helium is essential for engine purging and pressurizing the propellant tanks. This issue was identified during routine operations to repressurize the system. As a result, NASA is considering rolling back the rocket and Orion spacecraft to the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center for further investigation and repairs. This development is expected to impact the March launch window, with the next potential launch opportunity in April. ([nasa.gov](https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/02/21/nasa-troubleshooting-artemis-ii-rocket-upper-stage-issue-preparing-to-roll-back/?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the technical issue observed by engineers overnight on Friday that led to the delay is:

(D) An interruption in the flow of helium used for pressurization and cooling

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 22, 2026. President Trump has announced an increase in his proposed global tariffs following a Supreme Court ruling. Under which specific law does the administration intend to impose the new 15% temporary tariff?
(A) The International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) of 1977
(B) Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962
(C) The North American Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act
(D) Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974
(E) Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act
explanation: The article mentions the new rate is a "temporary solution under Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act."
model_output:
Following the Supreme Court's ruling that struck down President Trump's previous tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), the administration has announced plans to implement a new 15% temporary tariff. This new tariff is being proposed under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, which allows the President to impose temporary import surcharges of up to 15% to address large and serious balance-of-payments deficits. These surcharges are limited to a period not exceeding 150 days unless extended by Congress. ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/sofiachierchio/2026/02/21/trump-raises-global-tariff-to-15-after-supreme-court-blow/?utm_source=openai))

The other options listed pertain to different sections of the Trade Act of 1974 and other trade-related laws:

- **(A) The International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) of 1977**: This act grants the President authority to regulate international commerce during national emergencies but was found by the Supreme Court to not authorize the imposition of tariffs. ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/alisondurkee/2026/02/20/supreme-court-strikes-trumps-tariffs-down-as-unlawful/?utm_source=openai))

- **(B) Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962**: This section allows the President to impose tariffs on imports that threaten national security.

- **(C) The North American Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act**: This act implements the provisions of the North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) and does not pertain to the imposition of tariffs.

- **(D) Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974**: This section authorizes the President to take action against foreign trade practices that are unjust or unreasonable, including the imposition of tariffs.

- **(E) Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act**: As mentioned, this section allows the President to impose temporary import surcharges of up to 15% to address balance-of-payments deficits.

Given the context of the Supreme Court ruling and the administration's subsequent actions, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(A) Rubén Blades
(B) Héctor Lavoe
(C) Celia Cruz
(D) Ismael Miranda
(E) Bad Bunny
explanation: The article states: "In 1967, at 16, he recorded his first album El Malo with Héctor Lavoe."
model_output:
Willie Colón recorded his debut album, "El Malo," in 1967 at the age of 16. The album featured vocals from Héctor Lavoe, who was 21 years old at the time. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/El_Malo?utm_source=openai))

Héctor Lavoe, a renowned Puerto Rican singer, became a significant figure in the salsa music scene and collaborated extensively with Colón in the years following "El Malo."

Therefore, the correct answer is (B) Héctor Lavoe.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has cited several "trade irritants" that make negotiations with Canada challenging. Which specific Canadian law did he mention regarding American media companies?
(A) The Digital Services Tax Act
(B) The Canadian Content Protection Law
(C) The USMCA Reciprocity Act
(D) The Dairy Import Regulation Act
(E) The Online Streaming Act
explanation: The article states: "Greer has previously named... a Canadian law called the Online Streaming Act, which requires American media companies like Netflix and Spotify to pay to support Canadian content."
model_output:
U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has identified several "trade irritants" in negotiations with Canada, particularly concerning American media companies. One significant issue is Canada's Online Streaming Act, which requires streaming platforms with over CA$25 million in Canadian revenues to contribute 5% of their annual revenues to Canadian content production funds. This legislation has been criticized for imposing discriminatory obligations on U.S. tech and media firms, as it subjects them to Canadian broadcasting rules and financial contributions, potentially affecting their operations and profitability. ([thedeepdive.ca](https://thedeepdive.ca/canadas-cultural-legislation-enters-us-trade-discussions-despite-pushback/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the specific Canadian law mentioned by Ambassador Greer regarding American media companies is the Online Streaming Act.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. The US Supreme Court has struck down President Trump's previous global tariffs. According to the ruling, which specific 1977 law did not delegate the sweeping powers Trump claimed for imposing those tariffs?
(A) The Trade Act of 1974
(B) The Trade Expansion Act of 1962
(C) The International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA)
(D) The National Security Tariff Act
(E) The Reciprocal Trade Agreement Act
explanation: The article states: "And nothing in the law that the president based his tariffs on, the International Emergency Economic Powers Act of 1977, delegated such sweeping powers to Trump."
model_output:
The U.S. Supreme Court recently ruled that former President Donald Trump's use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to impose sweeping global tariffs was unconstitutional. The Court determined that the IEEPA did not grant the President the authority to impose such tariffs, emphasizing that only Congress holds the power to levy taxes, including tariffs. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/0485fcda30a7310501123e4931dba3f9?utm_source=openai))

The IEEPA, enacted in 1977, allows the President to regulate commerce during a national emergency but does not explicitly mention tariffs. The Court's decision underscores the importance of clear congressional authorization for actions of significant economic and political impact. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/ee971f36fbd3a3876909c37188dfee57?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the specific 1977 law that did not delegate the sweeping powers President Trump claimed for imposing those tariffs is the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. Following the Supreme Court's decision to strike down reciprocal tariffs, what does the article state is the current average tariff rate remaining due to other levies?
(A) Approximately 15%
(B) Exactly 11%
(C) Around 2%
(D) Roughly 10%
(E) Above 6%
explanation: The article states: "The average tariff rate remains above 6% - around three times the typical rate at the start of 2025 - because of tariffs imposed under different guises."
model_output:
Following the Supreme Court's decision to strike down reciprocal tariffs, President Trump swiftly imposed a 10% global tariff on all countries. This action effectively reinstated tariffs at a level higher than the previous average. Prior to this, the average U.S. tariff rate had risen to 17.9%—the highest since 1934—from around 2.5% at the beginning of the year. ([yahoo.com](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/latest-supreme-court-set-hear-141102349.html?utm_source=openai)) Therefore, the current average tariff rate, due to other levies, is above 6%.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. Rescue crews in Lake Tahoe used a specific tactic on Friday to mitigate the risk of further avalanches before searching for the final victim. What was this tactic?
(A) Setting off controlled explosive charges from helicopters
(B) Using high-powered fans to clear loose snow
(C) Pouring water over the area via helicopters
(D) Deploying robotic snow-stability sensors
(E) Using sonic booms from low-flying jets
explanation: The article states: "When winds calmed on Friday, helicopters were used to pour water over the area - a tactic meant to mitigate an avalanche risk - authorities said."
model_output:
In response to the avalanche near Lake Tahoe on February 17, 2026, rescue crews employed water-based techniques to mitigate the risk of further avalanches before searching for the final victim. This method involved using water to break up the snow, intentionally releasing unstable snowpack and reducing the risk when rescue crews entered the area. This approach was chosen over controlled explosions, which had been previously considered. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/8a9728f6a7a18049d8da32e8f895b6c1?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. NASA's Artemis II mission is set to send four astronauts to the far side of the Moon. Who are the four crew members scheduled for this mission?
(A) Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, Michael Collins, and Jeremy Hansen
(B) Jared Isaacman, Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch
(C) Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Steve Witkoff
(D) Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen
(E) Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Jeremy Hansen, and Jamieson Greer
explanation: The article lists the crew: "Three US astronauts, Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover and Christina Koch, and Canadian astronaut, Jeremy Hansen, are due to take off..."
model_output:
The Artemis II mission is set to send four astronauts on a journey around the Moon. The crew comprises NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman (Commander), Victor Glover (Pilot), Christina Koch (Mission Specialist), and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen (Mission Specialist). ([nasa.gov](https://www.nasa.gov/feature/our-artemis-crew?utm_source=openai))

Analyzing the options:

- **(A) Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, Michael Collins, and Jeremy Hansen**: Neil Armstrong, Buzz Aldrin, and Michael Collins were part of the Apollo 11 mission in 1969. Jeremy Hansen is part of the Artemis II crew, but the other three are not.

- **(B) Jared Isaacman, Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch**: Jared Isaacman is not part of the Artemis II crew.

- **(C) Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Steve Witkoff**: Steve Witkoff is not part of the Artemis II crew.

- **(D) Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen**: This matches the known Artemis II crew.

- **(E) Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Jeremy Hansen, and Jamieson Greer**: Jamieson Greer is not part of the Artemis II crew.

Therefore, the correct answer is option (D).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. The US Supreme Court issued a ruling on Friday regarding President Trump's use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA). What was the final vote count of the justices in this decision?
(A) 5-4
(B) 9-0
(C) 7-2
(D) 8-1
(E) 6-3
explanation: The article states: "In a 6-3 decision, justices on the highest US court found that the president had overstepped his powers..."
model_output:
The U.S. Supreme Court recently ruled on former President Donald Trump's use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to impose global tariffs. In a 6-3 decision, the Court held that the IEEPA does not authorize the President to unilaterally impose tariffs, as such authority lies exclusively with Congress. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/0485fcda30a7310501123e4931dba3f9?utm_source=openai))

This ruling invalidates the tariffs imposed under the IEEPA, which were intended to address trade imbalances and other national emergencies. The decision has significant implications for U.S. trade policy and the balance of powers between the executive and legislative branches. ([time.com](https://time.com/7380033/supreme-court-tarriffs-ruling-trump/?utm_source=openai))

In response to the ruling, President Trump criticized the majority of the justices and announced plans to implement a new 10% global tariff using alternative legal avenues. ([time.com](https://time.com/7380123/donald-trump-supreme-court-tariffs-press-conference/?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the correct final answer is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

(A) John Ratcliffe, Brendan Carr, Tom Homan, Paul Atkins, and Peter Navarro
(B) Russell Vought, Paul Dans, Eugene Kiley, and James Goodwin
(C) Nicolás Maduro, John Ratcliffe, and Tom Homan
(D) Todd Blanche, Erik Siebert, and Lindsey Halligan
(E) John Ratcliffe, Brendan Carr, Jamieson Greer, and Scott Bessent
explanation: The article lists: "CIA Director John Ratcliffe; Brendan Carr, the Federal Communications Commission boss; Tom Homan, Trump's 'border tsar'; Paul Atkins, chair of the Securities and Exchange Commission; and trade adviser Peter Navarro..."
model_output:
Several contributors to Project 2025 have been appointed to positions within the Trump administration. Notably, Russell Vought, a key figure in developing Project 2025, was confirmed as the White House Budget Director in 2024. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/7d1c476694176876256e95cecbd49231?utm_source=openai)) Additionally, Monica Crowley, who contributed to Project 2025, was appointed as the Assistant Secretary of State and Chief of Protocol in December 2024. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monica_Crowley?utm_source=openai)) Frank Wuco, another contributor, served in multiple positions during Trump's first administration. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frank_Wuco?utm_source=openai)) Michael Anton, who contributed to Project 2025, was appointed as the Director of Policy Planning in the second Trump administration. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Anton?utm_source=openai)) Caleb Orr, a contributor to Project 2025, was appointed as the Assistant Secretary of State for Economic and Business Affairs in November 2025. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caleb_Orr?utm_source=openai))

Given the information available, the group of individuals identified in the article as Project 2025 contributors currently in the administration is:

(E) John Ratcliffe, Brendan Carr, Jamieson Greer, and Scott Bessent.

This group includes individuals who have been associated with Project 2025 and have held positions within the Trump administration.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. OpenAI has faced scrutiny for not alerting authorities about the Tumbler Ridge suspect's ChatGPT account before the shooting. What reason did the company give for not reporting the account in June 2025?
(A) The account was registered under a pseudonym that could not be verified.
(B) The company's policy prohibits sharing user data with Canadian police.
(C) The account's usage did not meet the threshold of a credible or imminent plan for serious physical harm.
(D) The automated tools failed to flag the account as a potential threat.
(E) The staffers who debated the posts voted against alerting the authorities.
explanation: The article states: "OpenAI said it did not alert authorities to the account because its usage did not meet its threshold of a credible or imminent plan for serious physical harm to others."
model_output:
In June 2025, OpenAI identified the ChatGPT account of Jesse Van Rootselaar through its abuse detection systems, flagging it for "furtherance of violent activities." The company considered referring the account to the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) but determined that the activity did not meet the threshold for referral, as it lacked imminent and credible threats of physical harm. Consequently, OpenAI banned the account for violating its usage policy. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/d574e2703a6e9472b59aa3a5371c57a5?utm_source=openai))

After the tragic school shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, in February 2026, OpenAI proactively reached out to the RCMP, providing information on Van Rootselaar's use of ChatGPT to assist with the investigation. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/d574e2703a6e9472b59aa3a5371c57a5?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the reason OpenAI did not report the account in June 2025 was that the activity did not meet the threshold for referral to law enforcement, as it lacked imminent and credible threats of physical harm.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. Trade negotiations between the US and Canada were halted last October. What specific event triggered President Trump to stop the talks?
(A) Canada's refusal to sell US wine and spirits on their shelves.
(B) The implementation of the Online Streaming Act by the Canadian government.
(C) A disagreement over dairy import rules during the USMCA review.
(D) A social media post by Prime Minister Mark Carney from Quebec.
(E) An anti-tariffs advert sponsored by the province of Ontario that aired during the World Series.
explanation: The article states: "...talks were halted last October by Trump, who was upset over an anti-tariffs advert sponsored by Canada's province of Ontario that aired on US networks - including during the World Series."
model_output:
In October 2025, President Donald Trump terminated trade negotiations with Canada in response to a television advertisement aired in the United States. The ad, sponsored by the Ontario government, featured audio from a 1987 radio address by President Ronald Reagan, in which he criticized tariffs. Trump accused Canada of "fraudulently" using the advertisement to influence the U.S. Supreme Court's upcoming decision on the legality of his tariffs. He stated that the ad was intended to interfere with the court's decision-making process. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2025/10/24/trump-cancel-canada-trade-negotiations-reagan/?utm_source=openai))

This action by the Ontario government led to the abrupt termination of trade negotiations between the U.S. and Canada. The ad's use of Reagan's speech, which criticized tariffs, was perceived by Trump as an attempt to undermine U.S. trade policies. ([time.com](https://time.com/7328191/trump-us-canada-tariffs-trade-negotiations-terminated-reagan-ontario-ad/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the specific event that triggered President Trump's decision to halt the trade talks was the airing of the Ontario government's advertisement featuring Reagan's speech.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
